questions.question: A bookshelf has $15$ shelves, and each shelf can hold $18$ books. If the bookshop owner wants to pack the books in boxes that hold $3$ books each, how many boxes can be filled with $15 \times 18$ books?
What is the remainder when $43$ is divided by $9$?
If a bakery sells $252$ loaves of bread per day, and they are packed in bags of $7$ loaves each, how many bags are sold per day?
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have $48$ pieces of candy and there are $8$ friends, how many pieces will each friend get, and what will be the remainder?
What is the remainder when $2^{10}$ is divided by $5$?
A water tank can hold $240$ gallons of water. If $3$ buckets of water are poured into the tank per hour, and each bucket holds $5$ gallons, what fraction of the tank will be filled after $8$ hours?
A clock strikes $6$ times at $12$ o'clock. If it strikes $x$ times after $y$ hours, find $x$ when $y = 9$.
What is the remainder when $17$ is divided by $5$, and what is the quotient?

A person is planning a road trip that is $945$ miles long. If they drive $5$ hours per day at a constant speed of $45$ miles per hour, what is the total number of days required to complete the trip? Show all work and calculations.

A bakery has $288$ cupcakes to package for a large order. They want to put $8$ cupcakes in each box. How many boxes are needed, and what is the remainder? Explain the reasoning behind the calculation.

teacher_rules: Ensure students have scratch paper for calculations
Monitor students for adherence to the no-explanation rule
Remind students to check their work carefully before submitting
